reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
mechanism_type: concerted pericyclic (cycloaddition) with prior thermal nitrile oxide generation
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring (C3–O1 and C5–C4)
bond_broken: N–O (partial, nitroacetate dehydration to nitrile oxide), O–H (water elimination)

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are compatible at room temperature and only react at elevated temperature (120°C). Pre-mixing in DES before entering heated coil is safe and simplifies the setup. No premature reaction occurs at RT.

reagents.name: phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne serving as 2π component in cycloaddition

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure full conversion of alkyne

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with EG (1 mL total DES)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide formation without added base or catalyst

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; facilitates dehydration of nitroacetate to nitrile oxide

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to its aci-nitro form (nitronic acid): ethyl nitroacetate → ethyl α-nitrosoacetate enol tautomer
Dehydration of the aci-nitro (nitronic acid) form to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (the reactive 1,3-dipole). Water is eliminated. The DES medium facilitates this step.

Concerted [3+2] 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate. Regioselectivity favors 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole due to electronic and steric factors (oxygen attacks terminal carbon of alkyne).

stream_logic.reasoning: Single feed stream: all components are mutually compatible at room temperature. The reaction only initiates at 120°C, so premixing is safe. Using one stream simplifies the flow setup. Concentration of phenylacetylene ~0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate ~1.0 M in 1 mL DES. A single syringe pump delivers the premixed solution into the heated coil reactor.

mixing_order_reasoning: No mixing order constraints. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are stable together at room temperature in the DES. The nitrile oxide only forms at elevated temperature (120°C), so premixing all components before entering the heated zone is optimal. This also ensures the dipolarophile (phenylacetylene) is present when the nitrile oxide is generated, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species involved. The reaction proceeds thermally in air.
quench_reasoning: No quench needed inline. The batch protocol quenches with water for workup/extraction, but in flow this would be handled offline or via an inline liquid-liquid separator as part of downstream processing, not as a chemical quench requirement.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction at 120°C. No photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism and flow translation. The DES (TBAB/EG) is viscous, which may cause higher backpressure in narrow-bore tubing — a 1.0 mm ID coil is recommended. Residence time of ~15 min at 120°C should be sufficient. The viscosity of the DES at 120°C is significantly lower than at RT, mitigating flow issues. Uncertainty: exact viscosity of TBAB/EG at 120°C and whether a single stream or two streams is optimal — chose single stream since no premature reaction occurs at RT. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests some nitrile oxide dimerization is expected; in flow, the precise temperature control may allow reducing this excess.
